digest.component_or_area: rebar-mcp server auth framework (optional HTTP transport)
propositions: rebar-mcp is currently stdio-only with no HTTP transport and no authentication, so a hosted store cannot be safely reached by remote MCP clients.
The epic adds an optional Streamable-HTTP transport where rebar acts as an OAuth 2.1 Resource Server validating inbound bearer tokens and advertising RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata.
A composite TokenVerifier is the single audience/fail-closed choke point, rejecting any token whose resource is absent or does not equal the configured auth_resource_server_url.
Five verifier modes ship (static bearer, JWKS/JWT, RFC 7662 introspection, trusted-proxy passthrough, pluggable custom), each selectable via flat mcp.auth_strategies config.
Misconfiguration and downgrade fail closed: unknown strategy, empty composite with auth on, auth-enabled HTTP with no verifier, or unauthenticated HTTP without explicit ack all refuse to start.
The change is purely additive: stdio remains the default transport, auth is off by default, and rollback is leaving the new mcp.* keys unset.
